description:
A small collection of CSV files has already been placed in
    /home/user/data/raw/.  
    You are a machine–learning engineer who must prepare this information
    for a model-training pipeline.  
    Create three new artefacts and respect the *exact* directory / file
    names and the formats that follow ­– the automated grader will read
    them byte-for-byte:

    1. /home/user/data/combined/products_all.csv  
       • Must contain **every** row from both raw CSV files, with the same
         header line, and in the order “2021 rows first, then 2022 rows”.  
       • A final newline **is required**.

    2. /home/user/data/processed/products_clean.jsonl  
       • Build it from products_all.csv but  
         – drop every row whose discontinued column is "yes"  
         – convert product_name to lower-case  
         – keep **only** the four fields: id (int), product_name (string),
           price (float), category (string) – in that order.  
       • Each line must be a single JSON object (newline-delimited JSON).  
       • No extra spaces anywhere inside the JSON objects.  
       • A final newline **is required**.

       Example of one correct line (do **not** hard-code):  
       {"id":1,"product_name":"widget","price":19.99,"category":"gadgets"}

    3. /home/user/data/processed/prep.log  
       • Exactly four lines, each using the syntax  
         key: value  
         (keys and the colon are lowercase; one space after the colon).  
       • The first line MUST be  
         timestamp: 2023-04-01T12:00:00Z  
         (write it literally – do not calculate the date).  
       • The next three lines must be:  
         total_rows_original: <integer>  
         rows_after_filter: <integer>  
         unique_categories: <comma-separated-list-in-alphabetical-order>  
       • No extra blank lines, and a final newline **is required**.

    Hints (you are free to choose the exact commands):
      – Inspect the two raw CSV files.  
      – Concatenate them to create products_all.csv.  
      – Use standard CLI utilities (e.g., cut, grep, awk, jq, python, etc.)
        to perform the filtering, lower-casing and JSON creation.  
      – Count rows and collect categories to complete prep.log.  

    The whole job can be done comfortably in 5–10 shell commands.
    Remember: paths, filenames and the exact formats are strict – tests
    will fail on even a single character difference.
truth:
<!-- Pre-existing data (already present before the agent starts) -->
  <preexisting_files>
    <file path="/home/user/data/raw/products_2021.csv"><![CDATA[id,product_name,price,category,discontinued
1,Widget,19.99,gadgets,no
2,Gizmo,29.99,gadgets,no
3,OldThing,9.99,antiques,yes
]]></file>

    <file path="/home/user/data/raw/products_2022.csv"><![CDATA[id,product_name,price,category,discontinued
4,Widget Pro,24.99,gadgets,no
5,Gizmo Plus,34.99,gadgets,no
6,AncientThing,49.99,antiques,no
]]></file>
  </preexisting_files>

  <!-- Files that the agent must create for a successful submission -->
  <expected_output_files>

    <file path="/home/user/data/combined/products_all.csv"><![CDATA[id,product_name,price,category,discontinued
1,Widget,19.99,gadgets,no
2,Gizmo,29.99,gadgets,no
3,OldThing,9.99,antiques,yes
4,Widget Pro,24.99,gadgets,no
5,Gizmo Plus,34.99,gadgets,no
6,AncientThing,49.99,antiques,no
]]></file>

    <file path="/home/user/data/processed/products_clean.jsonl"><![CDATA[{"id":1,"product_name":"widget","price":19.99,"category":"gadgets"}
{"id":2,"product_name":"gizmo","price":29.99,"category":"gadgets"}
{"id":4,"product_name":"widget pro","price":24.99,"category":"gadgets"}
{"id":5,"product_name":"gizmo plus","price":34.99,"category":"gadgets"}
{"id":6,"product_name":"ancientthing","price":49.99,"category":"antiques"}
]]></file>

    <file path="/home/user/data/processed/prep.log"><![CDATA[timestamp: 2023-04-01T12:00:00Z
total_rows_original: 6
rows_after_filter: 5
unique_categories: antiques,gadgets
]]></file>

  </expected_output_files>
